[Wed 2026-03-18 12:52 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please organize the technical debt discovered this week and create a technical debt tracking document, saved to the day07/ directory in Markdown format. Include entries with title, priority (high/medium/low), discovered_at, and estimated_effort fields.

arguments.content:
# Technical Debt Tracking

**Document Created:** March 24, 2026  
**Team:** Backend Engineering  
**Sprint:** 8  

---

## Overview

This document tracks technical debt items identified during Sprint 8 planning and the API Review Meeting held on March 23, 2026. Each entry includes prioritization and estimated effort for sprint planning purposes.

---

## Active Technical Debt Items

### TD-001: v1 API Endpoint Maintenance Burden

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | Legacy v1 API endpoints use outdated authentication and inconsistent patterns |
| **Priority** | High |
| **Discovered At** | March 23, 2026 (API Review Meeting) |
| **Estimated Effort** | 160 hours (2 engineers × 2 weeks) |

**Description:**  
v1 endpoints currently rely on legacy API key authentication and inconsistent error response formats. Maintenance requires working with deprecated libraries and manual security patches.

**Impact:**
- Security vulnerabilities in legacy auth flow
- Developer time consumed by patches instead of feature work
- Cannot leverage modern infrastructure (rate limiting, monitoring)

**Remediation Plan:**  
Deprecate endpoints by June 30, 2026. Migrate clients to v2.3. Track in Sprint 8-12.

---

### TD-002: Lack of Unified Authentication Mechanism

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | Multiple authentication systems (API keys, basic auth, legacy tokens) without single sign-on |
| **Priority** | High |
| **Discovered At** | March 23, 2026 (API Review Meeting) |
| **Estimated Effort** | 120 hours |

**Description:**  
Current infrastructure supports multiple auth mechanisms simultaneously, increasing attack surface and operational complexity. No centralized identity provider.

**Impact:**
- Security audit failures
- Complex user permission management
- Duplicate user credential storage

**Remediation Plan:**  
Implement OAuth2 + JWT as single auth standard in v2.3. Sunset other methods by Q3 2026.

---

### TD-003: Hardcoded Rate Limiting

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | Rate limiting logic embedded in application code without tiered support |
| **Priority** | Medium |
| **Discovered At** | March 23, 2026 (API Review Meeting) |
| **Estimated Effort** | 80 hours |

**Description:**  
Current rate limiting is implemented per-endpoint with static limits. No infrastructure to support free vs. paid tier differentiation. Limits require code changes to modify.

**Impact:**
- Cannot support tiered pricing model
- Uneven resource allocation
- Customer complaints about rigid limits

**Remediation Plan:**  
Implement Redis-backed sliding window rate limiter in Sprint 8 (Module 2). Deploy with v2.3.

---

### TD-004: v2.2 Compatibility Bridge Technical Complexity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | Legacy auth-to-OAuth2 translation layer adds maintenance burden during transition |
| **Priority** | Medium |
| **Discovered At** | March 23, 2026 (API Review Meeting) |
| **Estimated Effort** | 60 hours (initial) + 20 hours/month maintenance |

**Description:**  
v2.2 compatibility bridge enables gradual migration but introduces technical debt in form of auth adapter code that must be maintained alongside new OAuth2 implementation.

**Impact:**
- Dual maintenance of auth systems during 3-month window
- Risk of security gaps in translation layer
- Testing matrix complexity increases

**Remediation Plan:**  
Schedule v2.2 retirement for June 30, 2026. Mark as resolved post-deprecation.

---

### TD-005: Insufficient API Version Migration Tooling

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | No automated tooling for client migration from v1 to v2.3 |
| **Priority** | Medium |
| **Discovered At** | March 24, 2026 |
| **Estimated Effort** | 100 hours |

**Description:**  
Migration from v1 to v2.3 requires manual endpoint updates, auth changes, and response format adjustments. No automated codemods or linting rules exist to assist clients.

**Impact:**
- Higher friction for client migrations
- Support ticket volume increase expected
- Extended deprecation timeline risk

**Remediation Plan:**  
Develop migration CLI tool and IDE plugins in Sprint 9-10. Provide before June 30 deadline.

---

### TD-006: Sunset Header Implementation Gaps

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | Deprecated endpoints lack standardized deprecation headers |
| **Priority** | Low |
| **Discovered At** | March 23, 2026 (API Review Meeting) |
| **Estimated Effort** | 40 hours |

**Description:**  
Current deprecated endpoints do not consistently return `Deprecation` and `Sunset` HTTP headers, making programmatic detection of API changes difficult for clients.

**Impact:**
- Clients cannot auto-detect endpoint deprecation
- Poor developer experience
- Potential surprise breakages at sunset date

**Remediation Plan:**  
Implement `DeprecationMiddleware` in Sprint 8 (Module 3). Add to all v1 and v2.2 endpoints.

---

### TD-007: Redis Token Storage Security

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Title** | JWT token blacklist in Redis lacks encryption at rest |
| **Priority** | Medium |
| **Discovered At** | March 24, 2026 |
| **Estimated Effort** | 32 hours |

**Description:**  
Current design stores revoked JWT token identifiers in Redis plaintext. If Redis is compromised, token metadata is exposed.

**Impact:**
- Security compliance gap
- Potential information leakage

**Remediation Plan:**  
Enable Redis encryption at rest. Hash token identifiers before storage. Target Sprint 8 release.

---

## Debt Summary by Priority

| Priority | Count | Total Effort (hours) |
|----------|-------|---------------------|
| High | 2 | 280 |
| Medium | 3 | 272 |
| Low | 2 | 72 |
| **Total** | **7** | **624** |

---

## Sprint 8 Commitments

The following debt items are scheduled for resolution in Sprint 8:

- [x] **TD-001** — Partial (deprecation timeline established)
- [x] **TD-002** — Partial (OAuth2 spec finalized, implementation begins)
- [x] **TD-003** — Full resolution (Rate limiting engine module)
- [x] **TD-006** — Full resolution (Deprecation middleware in Module 3)
- [ ] TD-004 — Monitoring (compatibility bridge deployed with tech debt acknowledged)
- [ ] TD-007 — Full resolution (Redis encryption setup)

---

## Resolution Targets

| Quarter | Items to Resolve |
|---------|------------------|
| Q2 2026 | TD-003, TD-006, TD-007 |
| Q3 2026 | TD-001, TD-002, TD-004 (post-v1-sunset) |
| Q4 2026 | TD-005 |

---

*Document maintained by: Alex Zhang, Backend Tech Lead*  
*Next Review: April 6, 2026 (end of Sprint 8)*
